diabetes.dataset_description: UCI 130-US Hospitals Diabetes dataset. Predicts hospital readmission for diabetic patients. Contains many categorical medical codes (admission_type_id, discharge_disposition_id, admission_source_id) and medication columns with values like 'No', 'Up', 'Down', 'Steady'. Leakage columns encounter_id and patient_nbr already removed.
must_do: encode_categorical with one_hot for nominal columns
must_not_do: Do NOT use ordinal encoding on nominal categories
diabetes.notes: Dataset domain: medical/clinical. Categorical nature: mix of nominal (race, gender) and ordinal-like (medication dosage changes: No/Steady/Up/Down). High-cardinality columns: diag_1, diag_2, diag_3 (ICD codes, very high cardinality). ML model encoding guidance: One-hot encoding is strongly preferred. KNN uses Euclidean distance — ordinal encoding of nominal features makes distance meaningless (distance between 'race=1' and 'race=3' would be 2, implying they're more different than 'race=1' and 'race=2'). One-hot preserves equal distances between categories.
